system_description.overview: Janet is an AI-powered support ticket and customer service platform that automates ticket triage, provides intelligent chat assistance, and integrates with project management tools like Jira. It uses AI agents to route, prioritize, and resolve customer support workflows.

products.Ticket Kanban Board with Automated Ticke: AI-driven ticket management and routing interface
products.Intelligent Chat Assistant: AI chatbot for customer support interactions
products.Jira Actions: Integration layer for Jira project management workflows

infrastructure.cloud_provider: Supabase (carve-out — Supabase is a subservice organization providing database and infrastructure)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Single VPC with segmented subnets; network ACLs enforced
network_architecture.firewalls: Network ACLs and security groups within VPC
network_architecture.tls: TLS / HTTPS for all ingress traffic
network_architecture.topology: Internet → HTTPS Ingress → Janet AI API (Next.js) → Agent Engine (Worker/Celery) → Prisma Client (ORM) → Supabase (DATABASE_URL, external subservice)

compute: Not named — likely containerized or serverless given stack
application_architecture.environments: Separate environments (production isolated); specifics not detailed

authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for admin/privileged accounts and SCM access

ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Merge to main requires approval; branch protection rules enforced
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool with baseline configs as code
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Patch management policy; regular patching cycle

security_tools.antivirus: Antivirus installed on all servers and desktops; weekly AV definition updates

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — physical access controls delegated to Supabase (subservice)
A1.2 — environmental protections delegated to Supabase and building management (subservice)
notable_controls: All 5 trust criteria in scope (Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, Privacy) — broader than typical Type 1
Carve-out method applied for Supabase as subservice organization

infrastructure.rationale: Single VPC, multi-AZ via Supabase. No named cloud provider directly controlled by Janet. Heavy delegation to Supabase subservice. No CDN, no multi-region.

application_architecture.rationale: Named tech stack (Next.js, Celery, Prisma) shows maturity. Branch protection and code review enforced. No named compute platform or testing framework.

data_layer.rationale: Supabase PostgreSQL with daily backups and KMS encryption. No caching layer, no data warehouse, no named file storage. Data layer entirely delegated to subservice.

security_posture.rationale: Monthly vuln scans, annual external pen test, antivirus, MFA, IDS/IPS via Supabase. No WAF directly controlled by Janet; security partially delegated to subservice.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC baseline configs, branch protection, named frameworks (Next.js, Celery, Prisma). CI/CD tool and container orchestration not named.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy, annual review and testing, daily backups, multi-AZ via Supabase. No RTO/RPO stated. DR capability depends on Supabase SLAs.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Critical single-vendor dependency on Supabase for database, authentication, IDS/IPS, and infrastructure. Supabase failure would be existential. Only other named vendor is Jira integration.

overall.rationale: SOC 2 Type 1 with all 5 trust criteria and clean opinion is positive. However, critical concentration on Supabase, no WAF, undisclosed RTO/RPO, and limited direct infrastructure control create meaningful risk.

red_flags: Critical single-vendor dependency on Supabase for database, auth, IDS/IPS, and infrastructure — existential concentration risk
No WAF directly controlled by Janet
company_db metadata lists 'Render' as infra_provider — contradicts report which clearly shows Supabase as primary infrastructure
yellow_flags: CI/CD tool and container orchestration not named
green_flags: All 5 trust criteria in scope — most comprehensive SOC 2 engagement
Named, modern tech stack (Next.js, Celery, Prisma) shows engineering sophistication
Quarterly access reviews with 1-business-day revocation SLA
key_observations: Supabase as subservice means Janet does not directly control its own database infrastructure — carve-out method requires reliance on Supabase's own SOC 2 report
All 5 trust criteria included in a Type 1 report is unusual and suggests a mature compliance posture or customer-driven requirement
Celery/Worker agent engine suggests async AI task processing architecture
Prisma ORM suggests TypeScript/Node.js backend despite Next.js being listed as 'API' — full-stack JS architecture likely
ACCORP Partners template — same structure as Doe Labs in this batch
